title: The Shape of the Year
intro: Three natural movements. January to May is the seeker finding their own ground. June to August is the soul's encounter with Presence. September to December is the life that has been touched carrying itself forward.

movements.title: Finding the Ground
movements.blurb: The seeker finding their own ground — gratitude, aspiration, walk, fruit, sight.

movements.title: The Encounter with Presence
movements.blurb: What abides, the silence in which it is felt, and the emergence that shows it forth.

movements.title: Carrying It Forward
movements.blurb: The daily work, the quiet battle, the descent of grace, and the yielded completion.

mirror.note: January's Gratitude is the opening; December's Gratitude Returned is the closing that is also, quietly, the next opening.

months.name: January
months.note: The year opens. Gratitude as the ground from which the arc unfolds.

months.name: February
months.note: The flame lit on the gratitude soil — yearning, longing, the first call inward.

months.name: March
months.note: The seeker begins to walk. Peace as the ground of progress, not its opposite.

months.name: April
months.note: First fruit of the walk — the fresh life that follows steadiness.

months.name: May
months.note: The seeker now sees. Discernment as the Buddha's particular gift to the path.

months.name: June
months.note: After sight, the encounter — what abides when seeing falls quiet.

months.name: July
months.note: Presence is felt only in silence. The year's quietest issue.

months.name: August
months.note: The triple anchor — heaviest issue of the year. Three threads held as one emergence.

months.name: September
months.note: After the high emergence — the return to ordinary days. The Work is what sustains.

months.name: October
months.note: The inner dharma. Dussehra read inward — the battle is not against another but within.

months.name: November
months.note: Three anchors held together as one descent — the heaviest issue of the second half.

months.name: December
months.note: The closing that mirrors January — but in a different key. Gratitude is no longer a response to gifts received; it is a recognition that the entire year was grace.
